page: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare-metal server configured with an AMD EPYC 9254 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. This system delivers 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.9 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 960 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top tier for several key metrics. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top 10% performance in stress-ng single-core tests and top 25% in multi-core execution. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 10% and uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 296.64 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the system demonstrates top 10% capabilities in single-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong top 25% performance in Redis SET operations and database transactions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and memory bandwidth without the virtualization overhead of standard cloud instances. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads. It is optimized for database hosting, in-memory caching, data processing pipelines, and moderate language model inference tasks that benefit from fast DDR5 memory and high multi-core throughput.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare-metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 9254 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz and a 960 GB local SSD, this configuration provides a high-density memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmark results place its single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory score in the top 10%, with strong multi-core and database performance in the top 25%. Lacking a GPU, the server relies on its high-speed memory bandwidth and advanced vector extensions for processing. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, database operations, Redis caching, and text-generation workloads.
og_description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare-metal server featuring a 24-core AMD EPYC 9254 CPU, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and single-core performance for database workloads.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining 48 vCPUs with 384 GB of DDR5 memory for demanding database and memory-intensive workloads.
